items.headline: Global Markets Decline Amid Inflation Fears and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a significant sell-off, with major indices closing sharply lower as hotter-than-expected inflation data and escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East fueled concerns. The Producer Price Index (PPI) for February rose more than anticipated, while surging crude oil prices further intensified inflation worries. The Federal Reserve, after holding interest rates steady, acknowledged increased uncertainty due to these factors, impacting market sentiment. The S&P 500 notably broke below its 200-day moving average, reaching its lowest point in four months. (Not financial advice)

items.headline: Five Below Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Shares surged after the retailer reported a strong Q4 beat with robust revenue and adjusted EPS, along with optimistic guidance.

items.headline: Alcoa Plunges
items.summary: Alcoa experienced a sharp decline in its stock price today.

items.headline: MicroStrategy Tumbles
items.summary: The Bitcoin treasury company's stock tumbled.

items.headline: SoundHound AI Sinks on CFO Resignation
items.summary: SoundHound AI shares fell following the announcement of its CFO's resignation, adding to investor uncertainty.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads as Broad Market Declines
items.summary: Eight out of 11 S&P 500 sectors closed in negative territory. The Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) was a notable outperformer, rising 1.1% amidst surging oil prices. Conversely, sectors like Materials (-1.74%), Technology (-1.24%), Consumer Discretionary (-0.92%), and Financials (-0.90%) experienced significant declines.

items.headline: Fed Holds Rates Steady, PPI Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: The Federal Reserve concluded its March meeting by keeping the federal funds rate unchanged at 3.50%-3.75% for the second consecutive meeting. Fed officials now anticipate only one rate cut in 2026, and the inflation outlook for 2026 was revised higher to 2.7%. Meanwhile, the Department of Labor reported that the Producer Price Index (PPI) for February rose 0.7%, surpassing the 0.4% consensus estimate, with the core PPI increasing by 0.3%. Both year-over-year headline PPI (3.4%) and core PPI (3.9%) remain above the Fed's 2% target.

items.headline: Micron and Accenture Report Earnings
items.summary: Micron ($MU) reported impressive fiscal Q2 earnings, with EPS of $12.20 and revenue of $23.86 billion, significantly beating estimates. However, shares fell due to concerns over increased capital expenditure guidance. Accenture ($ACN) also announced strong Q2 fiscal 2026 results, including record new bookings of $22.1 billion and revenues at the top end of its guided range, while projecting 3% to 5% revenue growth for the full fiscal year 2026.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: The ongoing intensification of the Middle East conflict has led to a significant surge in crude oil prices. Brent futures settled up 3.8% at $107.38 per barrel, and U.S. benchmark West Texas Intermediate (WTI) futures rose 0.4% to $96.32 per barrel. This escalation in energy prices is contributing to global inflation concerns and impacting central bank policy expectations.
